title: European Consortium QUARTERNEXT Launches to Advance Certifiable Quantum-Key Distribution Systems - Quantum Computing Report
gist: A four-year EU consortium QUARTERNEXT, coordinated by Spain's Luxquanta and funded under the Digital Europe Programme's IRIS2 QCI framework, aims to miniaturize, certify and deploy Continuous-Variable QKD systems over existing fiber to advance the EuroQCI mandate and European technological sovereignty.

insights.claim: The stated gap for quantum-safe communication is not proving key exchange works but achieving strict regulatory certification and operating over existing classical fiber without signal degradation.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Certification and fiber-coexistence, not physics, are the commercialization gate for QKD—watch which vendors clear it first.
insights.quote: translating these frameworks into critical infrastructure requires strict regulatory compliance
insights.locator: The technological roadmap targets the miniaturization

insights.claim: QUARTERNEXT will feed prototypes into Nostradamus, the EU's designated infrastructure for testing and certifying quantum-safe systems, establishing a unified European security standard.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A single EU certification bottleneck (Nostradamus) will gate market access for QKD vendors.
insights.quote: the EU’s designated infrastructure for testing, evaluation, and certification of quantum-safe systems
insights.locator: To secure final European market deployment

insights.claim: The consortium anchors design and manufacturing in PIXEurope, the EU's €400M photonic integrated circuit pilot line, explicitly to build a domestic supply chain and secure European technological sovereignty in high-security components.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: QKD is being used as a lever for EU semiconductor/photonics sovereignty—a policy tailwind for European PIC suppliers.
insights.quote: aims to solidify European technological sovereignty and build a resilient, domestic supply chain
insights.locator: Furthermore, by anchoring its manufacturing and design loops

insights.claim: Coexistence frameworks that partition light frequencies let fragile quantum channels and heavy classical streams share the same physical fiber, minimizing installation overhead for telecom carriers—a key adoption enabler.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Fiber coexistence removes the need for dedicated dark fiber, cutting the cost barrier for carrier QKD rollout.
insights.quote: allowing fragile quantum data channels and heavy classical streams to run concurrently over the same physical optical fibers
insights.locator: To minimize installation overhead for commercial telecommunications carriers

insights.claim: The value chain is decomposed across specialist SMEs—Quside (QRNG entropy chips), Chilas (narrow-linewidth tunable photonic lasers), and fragmentiX (information-theoretic secret-sharing appliances)—each supplying a distinct hardware block.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Names the specific European component vendors positioned to supply the QKD stack.
insights.locator: The collective engineering execution integrates specialized hardware
